goal: Discover and validate three orthogonal feature families that generate new trades beyond the active-signal-v1 baseline, keeping all three workers saturated with parallel discovery-then-build work.
global_constraints: every slice must start with cheap data-availability discovery before any feature engineering
new features must target signal regimes the baseline misses: sentiment, order-flow, and cross-timeframe divergence
no slice may assume a feature family or dataset exists without first confirming via catalog or preview

slices.title: Funding rate and open-interest sentiment features
slices.hypothesis: Extreme funding rates and rapid OI changes reflect crowded positioning that precedes price reversals or accelerations the baseline does not capture, providing genuinely new entry signals.
slices.objective: Discover available funding and OI event/feature data, then engineer sentiment-extreme features and validate their signal independence from baseline trades.
success_criteria: Confirm at least one funding-rate or open-interest data source is available and fresh via events_sync or features_catalog
Produce one or more custom features (e.g. funding_zscore, oi_velocity) with non-degenerate distributions via features_analytics
Demonstrate via cheap correlation check or quick backtests_study that new features fire in time windows where baseline has zero trades
evidence_requirements: events or catalog listing proving funding/OI data availability and date range
distribution statistics for engineered features showing non-trivial variance
overlap analysis showing fewer than 30% of new-feature signals coincide with baseline trade windows

slices.title: Volume delta and trade-intensity microstructure features
slices.hypothesis: Order-flow imbalance captured by buy-vs-sell volume delta and trade intensity spikes reveals aggressive positioning shifts that pure price-based baseline signals miss, unlocking new trade opportunities during consolidation and breakout regimes.
slices.objective: Inventory existing volume-related features, identify gaps, engineer volume-delta and intensity features, and validate that they produce signals in under-traded regimes.
success_criteria: features_catalog and datasets_preview confirm volume or trade-level data coverage on BTCUSDT at 5m granularity
Engineer at least two features: cumulative_volume_delta and trade_intensity_ratio with healthy spread via features_analytics
Quick backtests_study or research_record shows new volume features trigger in low-baseline-activity periods (e.g. flat market breakouts)
evidence_requirements: catalog or dataset listing confirming volume-trade data availability
feature distribution report showing skew and kurtosis within usable bounds
regime overlap check: volume-feature signals must activate in at least 20% of bars where baseline is silent

slices.title: Cross-timeframe momentum and volatility divergence features
slices.hypothesis: When 5m momentum diverges from 1h momentum direction or when 5m volatility expands while 1h remains compressed, the market is entering a regime transition that the baseline's single-timeframe logic does not trade, yielding new entries at trend inflection points.
slices.objective: Discover available multi-timeframe feature building blocks, construct divergence features between 1h anchor and 5m execution frames, and validate orthogonality against baseline trade windows.
success_criteria: features_catalog reveals at least momentum and volatility indicator primitives at both 1h and 5m resolutions
Engineer at least two features: momentum_divergence_1h_5m and vol_expansion_ratio with non-degenerate distributions
Cheap backtests_study or features_analytics correlation confirms divergence signals overlap baseline trades less than 25% of the time
evidence_requirements: catalog listing confirming momentum and volatility features at both 1h and 5m
distribution and stationarity statistics for divergence features
signal overlap report quantifying co-occurrence with baseline trade timestamps
